commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The protected maintainer label, security-sensitive approval boundary, and choice between coverage-only refresh versus a new detector contract need maintainer handling rather than an automated repair lane.; Needs attention: The diff has no supply-chain issue, but it changes a security-sensitive exec approval detector in a way that duplicates current-main behavior and needs maintainer attention before merge.; R... (sha=07357d6ffeaa5ed757b0ae65f700b86764f7a590)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes strict inline-eval interpreter matching and adds regression coverage for versioned Python/PyPy, PHP inline flags, Rscript eval, gateway denial, and strict allow-always persistence.

PR surface: Source +13, Tests +120. Total +133 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source and related proof: the original gap was exact interpreter matching feeding strictInlineEval, and https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/96216 already demonstrates the fixed node-host denial and one-shot allow-always path. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The protected `maintainer` label, security-sensitive approval boundary, and choice between coverage-only refresh versus a new detector contract need maintainer handling rather than an automated repair lane.

target.title: test: cover versioned inline eval approval [AI]
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The strict inline-eval approval boundary needs source-level and gateway-path coverage for version-suffixed interpreter names and inline flags so those command forms continue to require explicit approval under `strictInlineEval=true`.

## Why This Change Was Made

This makes versioned interpreter matching explicit in the inline-eval detector specs for the affected interpreter families, adds direct detector coverage for a versioned PHP binary, and broadens approval-path regression coverage for the reported carrier forms. It also verifies those carriers do not become reusable allow-always entries in strict mode.

This PR was AI-assisted.

## User Impact

Operators keep the same strict inline-eval guardrail behavior for interpreter inline-code commands. The change is limited to detector matching and regression coverage; it does not add config, migration, provider, plugin, channel, storage, or protocol behavior.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/command-analysis/inline-eval.test.ts src/infra/command-analysis/risks.test.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-allow-always.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-gateway.test.ts`
target.author: pgondhi987

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
